text.title: Outcome Based Roadmaps Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Outcome Based Roadmaps application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Roadmap rows MUST be outcomes (e.g. 'Reduce trial→paid friction'); feature names as rows are rejected.
rule.rationale: Outcome-first preserves optionality on how to achieve the outcome.

rule.statement: ≤3 outcomes per quarter; >3 is rejected as overcommitment.
Each solution MUST have a delivery window expressed as month-range (e.g. 'May-June'); exact-date promises are rejected.
rule.rationale: Exact-date promises lock the team and erode trust on miss.

rule.statement: Every solution MUST carry confidence (high/med/low) with cited evidence (discovery touchpoint id, experiment id); uncited confidence is rejected.
rule.rationale: Uncited confidence defaults to 100% and inflates the plan.

rule.statement: Quarter-end review MUST cite which outcomes shipped, which slipped, and the next quarter's adjustment; un-reviewed roadmaps are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without quarter-end review the roadmap becomes a feature wishlist.
